name: Melbourne
tagline: Coffee Culture, Sports Obsessed, and Endlessly Livable
description: Melbourne consistently ranks among the world's most livable cities, and for good reason. The city offers an extraordinary food and coffee scene, world-class sports events (Australian Open, F1, AFL), vibrant arts and music, and a relaxed yet cosmopolitan atmosphere. While housing costs are significant and the overall cost of living is high by global standards, Melbourne rewards residents with excellent public services, universal healthcare through Medicare, and a quality of life that few cities can match.

quickFacts.visa: Visitor visa (Subclass 600) allows stays up to 12 months. Investor Retirement visa (Subclass 405) closed to new applicants. Significant Investor visa requires A$5M+ investment. No easy retirement pathway — most retirees use long-stay tourist visas.
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – safe with typical urban precautions
quickFacts.climate: Mediterranean with warm summers and cool winters; famously changeable weather

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Footscray, Brunswick West, or outer suburbs near a train line

dining.description: Cheap eats on Lygon Street or Victoria Street; coffee shop culture 3-4x/week

healthcare.description: Medicare covers most GP and hospital visits; basic private extras for dental

transportation.description: Myki card for tram, train, and bus; free tram zone in CBD

entertainment.description: Free galleries at NGV, beach trips to St Kilda, park barbecues, street art walks

1M.lifestyle: This budget is workable in Melbourne but requires discipline. You will live in the inner suburbs — Footscray, Brunswick West — and cook most meals at home, supplemented by the city's affordable multicultural cheap eats. Medicare covers basic health costs, and the free tram zone, free galleries at the NGV, and beach access at St Kilda help keep entertainment affordable. Melbourne's weather is famously changeable, with cool winters that are mild by Canadian standards but gray.

housing.description: 2BR apartment in Fitzroy, Carlton, or South Yarra with character

dining.description: Regular dining in Melbourne's laneway restaurants, brunch culture, wine bars

healthcare.description: Medicare plus private health insurance for shorter wait times and dental/optical

transportation.description: Myki pass plus occasional car-share or ride-hail; weekend drives on the Great Ocean Road

entertainment.description: AFL season tickets, comedy festival shows, gym membership, Yarra Valley wine trips

2M.lifestyle: A two-bedroom in Fitzroy, Carlton, or South Yarra puts you in Melbourne's most interesting neighborhoods. Regular dining at laneway restaurants, AFL season tickets at the MCG, a car for Great Ocean Road or Yarra Valley weekends, and private health insurance on top of Medicare. The budget is comfortable without being lavish. Melbourne's food, coffee, and cultural scene provide a genuinely rich daily life at this spending level.

housing.description: Spacious 3BR house in Richmond, Prahran, or a modern apartment in Southbank with river views

dining.description: Fine dining at hatted restaurants like Attica and Lume, regular wine bar visits, hosted dinner parties

healthcare.description: Premium private health insurance with top hospital choice and comprehensive extras

transportation.description: Owned car for regional trips, Myki for city travel, occasional domestic flights

entertainment.description: Australian Open tickets, F1 Grand Prix, premium gym, sailing on Port Phillip Bay

3M.lifestyle: A spacious house in Richmond or Prahran, or a modern apartment in Southbank with river views. Regular dining at restaurants like Attica, Australian Open and F1 tickets, premium private health insurance, and your own car for regional trips. Weekend drives to the Mornington Peninsula or Great Ocean Road are easy. Melbourne's food and cultural scene is deep enough to support this spending level well.

housing.description: Luxury 4BR home in Toorak, Brighton, or a premium penthouse in the CBD

dining.description: Regular dining at Melbourne's best restaurants, private chef, exclusive wine club memberships

healthcare.description: Top-tier private insurance with concierge GP service and international coverage

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, premium insurance, occasional first-class domestic flights

entertainment.description: Corporate box at MCG, Australian Open platinum passes, yacht club membership, art collecting

domesticHelp.description: Weekly housekeeper, gardener, occasional personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: Mornington Peninsula weekends, first-class travel, premium wine cellar, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: A large home in Toorak or Brighton, a weekly housekeeper, a nice car, and regular dining at Melbourne's best restaurants. Corporate box access at the MCG and Rod Laver Arena, business class domestic flights, and premium healthcare with concierge GP service. Regular trips to Mornington Peninsula wineries round out a comfortable upper-tier lifestyle in one of the most livable cities in the world.

housing.description: Grand estate in Toorak or a waterfront mansion in Brighton with harbour views

dining.description: Personal chef, private dining at Australia's finest restaurants, catered events at home

healthcare.description: VIP concierge medicine with international network access and executive wellness programs

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, dedicated driver, first-class international travel, charter flights

entertainment.description: Private boxes at MCG and Rod Laver Arena, art patronage, yacht ownership, international events

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff including housekeeper, chef, personal assistant, gardener, and pool maintenance

luxuryExtras.description: Vineyard investment in Yarra Valley, first-class international travel, fine art, philanthropy

10M.lifestyle: A grand home in Toorak or a waterfront property in Brighton, a cook who comes in several times a week, regular housekeeping, and first-class international travel. Private boxes at the MCG and Rod Laver Arena, concierge healthcare, and a driver available when needed. Melbourne is expensive, so this budget does get absorbed more fully than in smaller cities, but the lifestyle is less about visible luxury and more about convenience and never worrying about costs.
